page: Microsoft Azure D96nlds_v6 is a virtual machine belonging to the Dnldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel processor architecture. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type includes 5669 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It is designated as a network-optimized and low-memory configuration within its family, prioritizing high-density compute resources and local disk capacity over memory allocation. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

The D96nlds_v6 offers a high concentration of dedicated compute cores and substantial local storage capacity, making it cost-efficient for workloads that require significant processing power and local disk space without demanding a high memory-to-core ratio. The primary tradeoff is the low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases. Appropriate workloads include network-optimized applications, compute-heavy tasks, and distributed file systems that can operate within the 192.0 GB memory limit while leveraging the 5669 GB local disk.
description: Microsoft Azure D96nlds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dnldv6 server family, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance features 5669 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, positioning it as a network-optimized and storage-capable compute node. With no integrated GPU and a low-memory profile, this server is designed for compute-heavy and network-intensive workloads that require substantial local disk space but do not demand high memory capacity. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for high-density processing tasks, though the low memory-per-core ratio represents a key tradeoff for memory-bound applications. Appropriate workloads include network-optimized processing, distributed file systems, and compute-intensive tasks.

tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, low memory density, and substantial local storage for compute-heavy workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
